title: Dog Ate Avocado Pit: Risks, Emergency Steps, and Veterinary Advice
meta_desc: If your dog swallowed an avocado pit, find out why this is a medium-urgency situation and how to monitor for signs of a gastrointestinal blockage.
content_html:
What is the risk of an avocado pit?

Avocado pits are not only a choking hazard but also represent a significant risk for gastrointestinal obstruction. Unlike the soft flesh of the fruit, the pit is incredibly hard and cannot be digested by your dog's stomach acid. Furthermore, avocado pits contain a fungicidal toxin called persin; while dogs are more resistant to persin than other animals, the physical blockage is the most immediate and dangerous concern for canines.

Is it an emergency?

The urgency level for this situation is Medium. You should contact your veterinarian or an emergency animal clinic as soon as possible. While it may not require immediate life-saving intervention if your dog is currently acting normally, the pit can become lodged in the narrow passages of the digestive tract. If your dog is coughing, gagging, or having trouble breathing, this elevates to a high-priority emergency.

How a photo helps our triage team

Providing a photo of the avocado variety or a similar-sized pit from the same batch can be incredibly helpful for our veterinary team. It allows us to compare the size of the object to your dog's breed and weight. This visual aid helps us estimate the risk of the pit passing through the pylorus and helps the veterinarian decide if inducing vomiting is safe or if immediate diagnostic imaging is required.

faqs.question: Can I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: You should never induce vomiting without professional guidance. Because of its size and shape, an avocado pit can easily become lodged in the esophagus on the way back up, which is a life-threatening situation.

faqs.question: What symptoms of a blockage should I watch for?
faqs.answer: Keep a close eye out for repeated vomiting, lethargy, loss of appetite, abdominal pain, or an inability to keep water down, as these are signs of a total obstruction.

faqs.question: Will the pit dissolve in my dog's stomach?
faqs.answer: No, avocado pits are extremely durable and will not be broken down by stomach acid. They must either be vomited up (under vet supervision), passed in the stool, or surgically removed.
